recipes.title: One-Pan Lemon Garlic Chicken
steps: Pat 4 chicken thighs dry with paper towels and season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Place chicken thighs skin-side down and sear for 6 minutes until golden.
Flip the chicken and cook for another 5 minutes, then remove from the pan.
Lower the heat to medium and add 4 minced garlic cloves to the pan.
Stir the garlic for 30 seconds until fragrant, taking care not to burn it.
Pour in half a cup of chicken broth and the juice of one lemon.
Scrape the browned bits from the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon.
Return the chicken to the pan along with any resting juices.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 8 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Sprinkle chopped parsley over the top and serve immediately with the pan sauce.

recipes.title: Fluffy Buttermilk Pancakes
steps: Whisk together 1.5 cups of all purpose flour, 3 tablespoons of sugar, 1 teaspoon of baking powder, half a teaspoon of baking soda, and a pinch of salt in a large bowl until evenly combined.
In a separate bowl beat 2 eggs lightly, then whisk in 1.25 cups of buttermilk and 3 tablespoons of melted butter until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry mixture and gently fold with a spatula until just combined. A few small lumps are fine, do not overmix or the pancakes will be tough.
Let the batter rest for 5 minutes while you preheat a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Lightly grease the skillet with butter and pour a quarter cup of batter for each pancake.
Cook until bubbles form across the surface and the edges look set, about 2 minutes.
Flip carefully and cook the other side for another minute or so until golden brown.
Stack on a warm plate and serve with maple syrup, fresh berries, or a dusting of powdered sugar.

recipes.title: Tomato Basil Pasta
steps: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil.
Add 12 ounces of spaghetti and cook to al dente per the package, usually 9 to 10 minutes.
While the pasta cooks, heat 3 tablespoons of olive oil in a wide skillet over medium heat.
Add 4 thinly sliced garlic cloves and a pinch of red pepper flakes, swirling for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in a 28 ounce can of crushed tomatoes, a teaspoon of salt, and a pinch of sugar.
Simmer the sauce for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
Reserve half a cup of pasta water, then drain the pasta and add it directly to the sauce.
Toss for a minute, adding splashes of pasta water until the sauce clings nicely to every strand.
Tear in a generous handful of fresh basil leaves and stir to wilt.
Plate immediately and finish each bowl with grated parmesan and a drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.title: Quick Miso Soup
steps: Pour 3 cups of water into a small saucepan and add a 4 inch piece of kombu.
Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat, do not let it boil hard.
Remove the kombu just before bubbles form and add a small handful of bonito flakes.
Steep for 30 seconds off heat, then strain through a fine sieve to make a clean dashi.
Return the dashi to the pan and add half a block of silken tofu cut into small cubes.
Whisk 2 tablespoons of miso paste in a ladle of warm dashi until fully dissolved.
Stir the miso back into the pot, keeping the heat below a simmer to preserve flavor.
Top each bowl with sliced scallions and a few rehydrated wakame pieces and serve.
